title: notopleura standleyana
intro: Notopleura standleyana, also known as Standley's notopleura, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Notopleura standleyana has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential use in the management of malaria and other infectious diseases. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, with its leaves and stems being the primary sources of medicinal compounds.
